mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering immediate equity repricing. When the unemployment rate increases by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, the S&P 500 produces negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all post-2000 samples.

mechanism_narrative: Equity valuations expand when industrial production growth outpaces housing construction declines, indicating a broad-based economic stabilization. The joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST compresses recession probabilities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of forward return variance in out-of-sample testing, or the signal produces negative returns in over 60% of active periods.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, real GDP growth acts as a primary valuation anchor. When GDPC1 expands quarter-over-quarter, equity markets price in higher corporate cash flows, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero during GDP expansion quarters, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output reflects collapsing aggregate demand and supply chain disruptions, forcing immediate equity deleveraging. When the industrial production index drops below 95.0, the S&P 500 delivers negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the cross-sectional predictive power (Rank IC) remains below 0.01 across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Labor market normalization drives equity recovery when nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds the unemployment rate increase, confirming cyclical employment expansion. This joint labor market improvement reduces recession risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for Federal Reserve policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples. A sustained increase in the CPI index directly reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive during high-inflation periods, or the historical correlation between CPI changes and forward SPX returns exceeds 0.15 in magnitude across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: Credit market stress amplifies equity drawdowns when consumer credit balances contract alongside housing starts decline. During credit contraction phases, the joint drop in RSAFS and HOUST triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual-series signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 during periods of elevated VIX levels.
